items.headline: England walk into the Azteca cauldron — plus Brazil-Norway — as the last 16 rolls on
items.body: Two more win-or-go-home ties today. First Brazil face surprise package Norway just outside New York (4pm ET), then the nightcap is a proper heavyweight bout: Mexico host England at the legendary Estadio Azteca in Mexico City (7pm ET). Harry Kane's England topped their group, but there's no tougher place to play than a packed, roaring Azteca. The winners punch tickets to the quarterfinals.
items.say: Mexico play England at the Azteca tonight — that stadium is going to be absolutely deafening for a World Cup knockout. Brazil-Norway's the early one. You catching either?

links.text: ESPN: today's World Cup schedule & how to watch
Yahoo Sports: Round of 16 bracket & live updates

items.headline: Morocco bounce co-hosts Canada — the Atlas Lions roar into the quarterfinals
items.body: Canada's dream run is over, and they're the first host nation knocked out of this World Cup. After a quiet first half in Houston, Morocco flipped a switch: Azzedine Ounahi curled in a free-kick opener just after the break, added a second late on, and Soufiane Rahimi tucked away a stoppage-time third for a clinical 3-0 win. Morocco march on to face France in Boston on Thursday.
items.say: Morocco just knocked Canada out of their own World Cup, 3-0. Co-hosts going home — and Morocco get France next. They're the story of the tournament right now.

links.text: NBC Sports: Ounahi double sinks Canada

items.headline: Mbappé's ice-cold penalty sends France into the last eight
items.body: No fireworks needed — just Kylian Mbappé, cool as ever. France ground out a 1-0 win over a stubborn Paraguay in Philadelphia, the game settled by a Mbappé penalty around the 70th minute after a clumsy foul on Désiré Doué. It's France's fifth straight win at a single World Cup for the first time ever, and they'll meet Cinderella side Morocco in the quarters.
items.say: France beat Paraguay 1-0 — Mbappé just calmly slotted a penalty and that was that. They've got Morocco next in the quarters, which is a heck of a story.

links.text: Al Jazeera: France edge Paraguay — as it happened

items.headline: UAE take over the Tour — del Toro wins in Barcelona as Ayuso grabs yellow
items.body: Total team domination on the Montjuïc climbs. UAE Team Emirates ran the show on Stage 2, and superstar Tadej Pogačar actually gifted the stage win to his 22-year-old teammate Isaac del Toro — only the second Mexican ever to win a Tour de France stage. Del Toro's other teammate, Juan Ayuso, pulled on the yellow jersey, knocking Jonas Vingegaard off the top. Three teammates, one very good day.
items.say: The Tour had a wild one — Pogačar basically handed the stage win to his young teammate del Toro, a Mexican kid, and another UAE guy took the yellow jersey. Total team takeover.

links.text: Cyclingnews: UAE flex their muscle on Montjuïc

items.headline: Wimbledon's biggest day: all sixteen fourth-round matches on one packed Sunday
items.body: The grass court Slam crams both singles draws into the fourth round today, and the pick is a beauty — world No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka vs. a red-hot Naomi Osaka, who's been rolling (and stealing the show with her walk-on outfits). Elsewhere Jannik Sinner, Novak Djokovic and Coco Gauff all take to the lawns with quarterfinal spots on the line. Perfect lazy-Sunday viewing.
items.say: Wimbledon's got its huge day today — they play all sixteen last-16 matches at once. Sabalenka against Osaka is the one to watch. Great background tennis for a Sunday.

links.text: CBS Sports: fourth-round live updates & bracket
Olympics.com: Sunday's full order of play

items.headline: The Minions win the weekend — but it's a franchise-low opening
items.body: The little yellow guys took the July 4 box office, yet the number came in soft: 'Minions & Monsters' opened to roughly $64.5M over five days (about $39.5M for the three-day weekend) — the lowest launch in the whole Despicable Me/Minions run, and well short of 'Despicable Me 4's' $120M holiday bow two years ago. Still No. 1, still the family go-to — just not the usual monster debut.
items.say: The new Minions movie won the weekend but actually had the weakest opening of any of them. Still, if you've got kids, it's the easy Sunday matinee. You take yours yet?

items.headline: Deep Purple, 57 years in, drop 'SPLAT!' — and critics say they're in a new golden age
items.body: The rock legends behind 'Smoke on the Water' just put out their 24th studio album, and it's landing hard: 'SPLAT!' scored a strong 79 on Metacritic, with Louder calling it proof the band is 'enjoying yet another golden age.' The band bills it as their heaviest in years, led by the single 'Arrogant Boy.' Prime dad-rock fuel for the weekend.
items.say: Deep Purple put out a new album — like their 24th — and the reviews are actually great. The 'Smoke on the Water' guys still going strong. Crank it in the garage.

items.headline: 'King of the Hill' is coming back — Season 15 lands July 20
items.body: I tell you what: Hank Hill is returning. Hulu dropped the trailer for the 'King of the Hill' revival's new season, picking up with Hank and Peggy settling into retirement on Rainey Street while Bobby's all grown up and stressing over life as a restaurant entrepreneur. All ten episodes drop July 20, with Mike Judge and the original voice cast back. Comfort-TV nostalgia at its finest.
items.say: King of the Hill's coming back with a new season on the 20th — Hank and Peggy retired, Bobby running a restaurant now. The trailer's out and it looks just like you remember it.

links.text: Variety: 'King of the Hill' sets Season 15 date

items.headline: Duran Duran headline Hyde Park tonight — '80s icons close out the London weekend
items.body: The 'Rio' and 'Hungry Like the Wolf' hitmakers top the bill at British Summer Time in London's Hyde Park tonight, wrapping a star-studded run of headliners that included Garth Brooks, Maroon 5 and Mumford & Sons. Four-plus decades on, Duran Duran are still one of the great big-crowd bands — and a guaranteed sing-along for anyone who grew up in the MTV era.
items.say: Duran Duran are headlining Hyde Park tonight — still going strong after all these years. Throw on 'Rio' and tell me it doesn't hold up. Total '80s comfort music.

links.text: Time Out: Duran Duran at BST Hyde Park
